author: srrjrr
title: Very good but not excellent
content:
I mainly listen to Indian music.  The selection is good not excellent, especially for classical and regional.  But my main complaint is that in spite of such a large number of Indian customers they don’t seem to understand that most Indian pop music the singer is usually the composer. The song details seem to mostly list the composer as the main artist and the singer as the artist and often not even listing the 2nd singer for duets.  

I am surprised that Indian customers tolerate this - but not surprising as Indians always love things foreign. I will try jiosaavn after my yearly subscription ends.

author: Hthggghiggh
title: I want to love it but I'm not quite there
content:
Spotify is fantastic overall, but it does a couple of things that also occur on other podcast apps and these things drive me bananas. I don't know why they all want to do these things. First, when I'm listening to a podcast, then I want to go to the following episode in that same podcast when that episode is over. I can't stand being immersed in an episode and getting to the end and then all of a sudden I'm slammed into an entirely different podcast that I've never heard of and have no interest in. It's like facing a dystopian alternate 
reality. I know there's a way to change that in settings, but that's a pain, and the next episode in the series should be the default. Second, I hate when I've got 15 minutes or 30 minutes or an hour and 57 minutes left in the podcast I'm listening to and Spotify decides that it's time to go to the next episode. Sometimes I can only listen to two or three minutes of a podcast at a time. Sometimes it takes me a couple of weeks to finish a single episode of a podcast. That doesn't mean that I don't want to finish it. Then I have to go through all the trouble of getting on my phone and going to my app and finding the episode that I haven't finished.

author: Eva C.
title: Slower, but glad that Spotify isn’t advertising for ICE anymore
content: Spotify playback is really lagging: songs, artist profiles, playlists, and podcasts are all consistently loading much slower than they used to. For how much of a drag this is, I think premium subscriptions are overpriced. I’m very glad that Spotify ceased advertising ICE recruitment ads, because I boycotted Spotify as a result of that (and encouraged others to do the same!) Once Spotify stopped airing advertisements for ICE recruitment, I did return to the platform because I enjoy accessing my playlists and podcasts from the same app. That said, now that I’ve returned, user experience has diminished due to the slowness and decrease in playback quality. Also, I wish artists were paid fairly for their work!

author: savvyk3nny
title: Reached My Limit
content:
I’ve been using Spotify since 2020 and used to love it. I used to be very adamant about never changing music apps, but I’m at my limit. 30 seconds used to be LONG for ad breaks, but now they’ve been reaching two minutes. I also used to be able to listen to 5-7 songs straight without ads, but now it’s more 3-4–5 if I’m REALLY lucky. 

Music is one of my biggest coping mechanisms, however I cant even have 15 minutes to myself without an ad. In fact, recently, they’ve changed it so ads will play right after the first song when you decide to start playing music. Not to mention the additional non-audio ads that pop up around the app as well. 

Now, the contents of these ads is another topic entirely. I’m not a purist by any means, but I really don’t enjoy hearing ads sampling a suggestive piece of a smutty audiobook, ESPECIALLY when I’m with family. 

If I hate the ads so much, why don’t I just buy premium? I used to, but then the price rose. And again, and again, and again. It is now THIRTEEN U.S. DOLLARS (fine, $12.99) for a single person. That is ridiculous. 

Additionally, the yearly music wraps have significantly decreased in quality. They used to be fun and accurate, but now parts are very obviously incorrect, nor do they seem to capture activity from the entire year. This is a big disappointment because, as someone who prides themself in listening to a wide range of genres, I loved seeing which ones I had explored each year. However, I began to see less appear in my “wraps” despite knowing very well I had listened to more. Whether it’s due to Spotify shrinking/simplifying genre groups or a lack of effort, I may never know, but it’s frustrating regardless.

Originally I had contemplated one star, but the fact that Spotify does offer a free listening experience grants it another. Nowadays it feels like you have to give an arm and a leg just for the bare minimum, but thankfully this wide range of music isn’t behind a paywall… just yet.
